$comment: Reproducible-vendoring manifest for the offline Mermaid runtime (arch-6). The 3.3 MB runtime is coupled to the template, the network-denying CSP, the label sanitizer, the hosted index, and the demo builder — this file records where it came from and how to update it safely. The checksum is verified by __tests__/vendor-manifest.test.cjs.
updateProcedure: Download the pinned dist: curl -fsSL https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@<version>/dist/mermaid.min.js -o mermaid.min.js
Update version, bytes, sha256 (shasum -a 256), and source in this manifest.
Run the required compatibility gates below; only commit if all pass.
Regenerate the inlined demo (node scripts/build-explain-inlined-demo.mjs) and eyeball ER + flowchart + sequence diagrams in light and dark, offline.
compatibilityGates: __tests__/vendor-manifest.test.cjs — checksum + byte-size of mermaid.min.js match this manifest
__tests__/self-contained.test.sh — no external network loads; template/demo carry the marker; network-denying CSP present
mermaid.initialize({ securityLevel: 'strict' }) + mermaid.run() API used by the template must exist in the new version
